decision_summary: The chip peak temperature (89.26°C) exceeds the 85.0°C limit. The most effective primary lever is to increase the heat spreader conductivity to enhance lateral heat distribution away from the hot spot. A conservative 40% increase is proposed, staying well within the allowed 2.0x step ratio and material bounds.

changes.reason: Increasing spreader conductivity improves lateral heat spreading, reducing the localized temperature gradient under the chip and lowering the peak temperature.

expected_effects: Reduction in chip_max_temperature towards or below the 85.0°C constraint
More uniform temperature profile across the heat spreader layer
risk_notes: Diminishing returns may occur if the bottleneck shifts to the base plate conductivity or boundary conditions rather than lateral spreading.
High conductivity values approach the upper bound of the editable range; further iterations may require geometric changes if this step is insufficient.
